explicit_text: I'm originally from Sudan, specifically from Darfur in North Sudan. Sudan is the biggest country in Africa and has many different cultures and tribes. Life there was difficult due to ongoing conflicts and struggles for basic needs like food and health. Despite the challenges, Sudanese people are very generous and welcoming. I left Sudan because of these problems and sought refuge elsewhere.
implicit_text: I'm originally from a large African country, specifically from the Darfur region in its northern part. This country is the biggest on the continent and has many different cultures and tribes. Life there was difficult due to ongoing conflicts and struggles for basic needs like food and health. Despite the challenges, the people are very generous and welcoming. I left because of these problems and sought refuge elsewhere.

explicit_text: After leaving Sudan, I lived as a refugee in Egypt for about six years. In Egypt, I felt more free even though I didn't always feel safe. I worked there as a teacher and helped refugees through my own charity. Egypt was close to Sudan, so I could easily visit my family, which was very important to me. However, I eventually moved to the United States seeking a better life.
implicit_text: After leaving my home country, I lived as a refugee in a neighboring North African nation for about six years. There, I felt more free even though I didn't always feel safe. I worked as a teacher and helped refugees through my own charity. This country was close to where I came from, so I could easily visit my family, which was very important to me. However, I eventually moved to the United States seeking a better life.

explicit_text: I came to the United States as a refugee about seven years ago, hoping for a better life and more freedom. I settled in Utica, New York, where I initially struggled with language and adjusting to life here. Although I thought I would be able to visit my family easily, my paperwork has been held for years, which has been very frustrating. Still, I appreciate the safety here, even if it doesn't feel as free as I expected.
implicit_text: I came to the United States as a refugee about seven years ago, hoping for a better life and more freedom. I settled in a city in upstate New York, where I initially struggled with language and adjusting to life here. Although I thought I would be able to visit my family easily, my paperwork has been held for years, which has been very frustrating. Still, I appreciate the safety here, even if it doesn't feel as free as I expected.

explicit_text: Since arriving in the United States, I have lived in Utica, New York. My first impression was that it was clean and safe, which was comforting. However, not having my legal papers made me feel insecure at times. I started studying English and later nursing here, balancing family responsibilities with my education.
implicit_text: Since arriving in the United States, I have lived in a city in upstate New York known for its safety and cleanliness. My first impression was that it was clean and safe, which was comforting. However, not having my legal papers made me feel insecure at times. I started studying English and later nursing here, balancing family responsibilities with my education.

explicit_text: Currently, I am a nursing student at St. Elizabeth College of Nursing. I chose nursing because of my family's background in healthcare; my father was a doctor and my mother was a nurse. Although I had a degree from Sudan, I had to start over here and decided nursing was a practical path for work in the U.S. I hope to succeed and build a career in this field.
implicit_text: Currently, I am a nursing student at a college specializing in nursing education. I chose nursing because of my family's background in healthcare; my father was a doctor and my mother was a nurse. Although I had a degree from my home country, I had to start over here and decided nursing was a practical path for work in the U.S. I hope to succeed and build a career in this field.

explicit_text: Before starting nursing, I studied ESL and general education at Mohawk Valley Community College beginning in 2015. MVCC helped me improve my English and prepare for further studies. It was an important step in adapting to life in the U.S. and pursuing my goals.
implicit_text: Before starting nursing, I studied ESL and general education at a community college in the Mohawk Valley beginning in 2015. This college helped me improve my English and prepare for further studies. It was an important step in adapting to life in the U.S. and pursuing my goals.

explicit_text: I practice Islam, which is the religion of most people in North Sudan where I am from. We pray five times a day and follow rituals that include learning parts of the Quran. In Sudan, children start memorizing the Quran very young, but it has been harder for my daughter to keep up with this since moving to Egypt and then the U.S. Islam remains a central part of my life and identity.
implicit_text: I practice the religion common to most people in the northern part of my home country. We pray five times a day and follow rituals that include learning parts of the holy scripture. In my country, children start memorizing this scripture very young, but it has been harder for my daughter to keep up with this since moving to the neighboring country and then the U.S. This religion remains a central part of my life and identity.

explicit_text: In Sudan, there are both Muslims and Christians, with most people in North Sudan being Muslim and many in South Sudan being Christian. While I personally practice Islam, I recognize that Christianity is also an important religion for many Sudanese people. The religious diversity is part of the cultural fabric of Sudan.
implicit_text: In my home country, there are both followers of Islam and Christianity, with most people in the northern region being Muslim and many in the southern region being Christian. While I personally practice Islam, I recognize that Christianity is also an important religion for many people from my country. The religious diversity is part of the cultural fabric of my homeland.

explicit_text: The Quran is the holy book I use to practice my faith in Islam. In Sudan, children begin learning and memorizing the Quran at a very young age, sometimes as early as two or three years old. Knowing parts of the Quran is essential for performing prayers properly. Since moving to Egypt and then the U.S., it has been more challenging to maintain this tradition, especially for my daughter.
implicit_text: The holy book I use to practice my faith is central to my religion. In my home country, children begin learning and memorizing this scripture at a very young age, sometimes as early as two or three years old. Knowing parts of it is essential for performing prayers properly. Since moving to the neighboring country and then the U.S., it has been more challenging to maintain this tradition, especially for my daughter.

explicit_text: Surah Al-Maghrib is one of the Quranic chapters that we recite during our prayers. It is part of the daily prayer routine in Islam, including the Isha prayer. Knowing and reciting Surah Al-Maghrib is important for fulfilling religious obligations, and I have practiced this since childhood in Sudan.
implicit_text: One of the chapters of the holy scripture that we recite during our prayers is important in our daily prayer routine, including the night prayer. Knowing and reciting this chapter is important for fulfilling religious obligations, and I have practiced this since childhood in my home country.

explicit_text: The Isha prayer is one of the five daily prayers I perform as a Muslim. It is done after sunset and includes reciting parts of the Quran like Surah Al-Maghrib. This prayer has been part of my religious routine since I was young in Sudan, and I continue to observe it here in the U.S. despite the challenges of adapting to a new environment.
implicit_text: The night prayer is one of the five daily prayers I perform as a follower of my faith. It is done after sunset and includes reciting parts of the holy scripture like a specific chapter. This prayer has been part of my religious routine since I was young in my home country, and I continue to observe it here in the U.S. despite the challenges of adapting to a new environment.

explicit_text: After studying in Utica, I moved to San Jose, near Campbell, California. There is a growing Muslim community there, including mosques and Quran classes for children. The presence of these communities helps maintain cultural and religious connections for Muslims like me living in the U.S.
implicit_text: After studying in upstate New York, I moved to the San Jose area near a city in California. There is a growing Muslim community there, including mosques and scripture classes for children. The presence of these communities helps maintain cultural and religious connections for Muslims like me living in the U.S.

explicit_text: In Campbell, California, I noticed that the Muslim community includes Bosniaks who have their own mosque. This shows the diversity within the Muslim population here. The Bosniak mosque is one of several that support religious and cultural activities for Muslims in the area.
implicit_text: In the California city near San Jose where I live, I noticed that the Muslim community includes people from Bosnia who have their own mosque. This shows the diversity within the Muslim population here. The Bosnian mosque is one of several that support religious and cultural activities for Muslims in the area.
